content: - Marked the task artifact completed, ran Ralph task_switch.sh, and now I am collecting the final git state before add/commit/push.
- Staging all tracked and untracked changes now, including Ralph metadata, before creating the required task-finished commit.

- Completed story-29 task 03: flake-owned runner-image and verify-image outputs now back the local image contracts, novice flows, and long-lane local helpers; production Dockerfiles and the dead Dockerfile-shape helper were removed.
- Verified the final tree with make check, make lint, and make test after the last Dockerfile removal, marked the task completed with passes=true, ran task_switch.sh, committed, and pushed master at 6d4ff51.
- If a future turn continues story-29, the next logical step is task 04 to migrate GitHub Actions image publication off the removed Dockerfiles and onto these flake image outputs.

- After the push I noticed .ralph/current_tasks.md and .ralph/current_tasks_done.md changed again, so I am checking the exact diff before deciding whether to leave them or sync them in one last commit.

- Read the previous Ralph progress log and the current active/done task summaries.
- Confirmed the visible active backlog currently contains one smell task plus story-29 tasks 04 and 05 and story-30 task 01, while the done summary shows all visible bug tasks already pass.
- Refresh the task summary, verify chooser rules and blocker state, then write the selected task path into .ralph/current_task.txt and stop.

- Refreshed Ralph task summaries and re-read the chooser rules after the files changed again.
- Inspected each active candidate task directly; no active bugs remain, and I am now checking for any hidden blocked_by or priority tags before selecting.
- If nothing outranks it, choose the story-29 CI migration task next because it directly continues the active Nix image migration chain from the just-finished story-29 task 03.

- Refreshed Ralph task summaries and re-read the chooser rules after the files changed again.
- Inspected all four active candidates directly; there are no active failing bugs, no active meta-task, and no visible blocked_by or priority tags changing precedence among them.
- Selected story-29 task 04 next because the actual repo stack is a Rust workspace built by Nix with remaining GitHub workflow files still needing migration onto the new flake/crane image outputs before downstream story-29 and story-30 work makes sense.

- Read previous progress, current task pointer, and the required tdd/improve-code-boundaries skill files.
- Confirmed the active task is story-29 task 04: migrate CI to Nix-only.
- Reading the task artifact and related story files next to determine whether this turn must plan-and-quit or execute.

- Read the task details, prior story plan example, current flake outputs, and the GitHub workflow set that still rebuilds images through Dockerfiles.
- Confirmed this task begins from a planning state and needs a plan artifact before any implementation turn.
- Drafting a plan focused on replacing Dockerfile-driven workflow behavior with flake-native build/publish jobs, parallel arch publication, protected-secret scoping, and hosted GitHub log verification.

- Read the workflow-specific AGENTS note and the GitHub API auth-wrapper skill because this task requires hosted workflow verification rather than fake local workflow tests.
- Fetching the latest Actions run state now via /home/joshazimullah.linux/github-api-curl so the plan reflects the real current hosted behavior.
- After that I will write the task-04 smell set and plan artifact, mark the plan ready for execution, and stop as instructed for planning turns.

- Created the task-04 workflow-boundary smell set at .ralph/tasks/smells/2026-04-28-story-29-ci-to-nix-boundaries.md.
- Created the execution plan at .ralph/tasks/story-29-migrate-to-nix-crane/04-task-migrate-ci-to-nix-only_plans/2026-04-28-migrate-ci-to-nix-only-plan.md and flipped its marker to NOW EXECUTE.
- Grounded the plan in the real hosted state: publish-images run #75 on commit 78060cb163138d27896e3e4e065c77a93ce40964 failed on 2026-04-27T23:55:42Z, with validate-fast, validate-long, and publish-image jobs failing; next turn should execute the plan directly.

- Read the previous Ralph progress log, confirmed the active task pointer, and re-read the required tdd and improve-code-boundaries skill files from disk.
- Confirmed story-29 task 04 is active and previous progress says its plan artifact is already marked NOW EXECUTE, so this turn should execute rather than plan.
- Reading the task markdown and its referenced execution plan next, then I will inspect the relevant workflows and tests before making any edits.

- Read the active task, execution plan, workflow-specific AGENTS note, and the current workflow/flake files.
- Confirmed the main boundary smell: image metadata is duplicated in .github/workflows/image-catalog.yml while publish-images.yml still rebuilds through docker buildx instead of consuming flake-owned image outputs.
- The first hosted API attempt used the auth wrapper with the wrong argument shape and failed on host resolution; retrying with a full GitHub API URL now, then I will inspect existing runner-image and verify-image contracts before editing flake/workflows.

- Added a flake-owned github.publishImageCatalog output with typed image/platform metadata and matrix data.
- Replaced the GitHub workflow implementation: removed image-catalog.yml, rewrote publish-images.yml around Nix eval/build plus skopeo/docker manifest publication, and updated promote-image-tags.yml to consume the same flake catalog.
- Verifying the new archive publish contract locally now by building runner-image/verify-image and checking that skopeo can inspect the Nix-built docker-archive reference before I spend a hosted run.

- Local execution checks now pass for the changed surfaces: github.publishImageCatalog evaluates, runner-image/verify-image build as Nix docker archives, skopeo can inspect the docker-archive transport, and actionlint passes on both workflows.
- Removed the workflow-owned image catalog, switched publish/promote workflows to flake-evaluated metadata, Nix-built image archives, Quay security gating from structured JSON artifacts, and docker-manifest plus skopeo publication instead of docker buildx rebuilds.
- Preparing a temporary push commit on master now so the real hosted publish-images workflow can run against these changes; after that I will inspect the exact Actions run via github-api-curl and fix anything that fails.

- Pushed temporary execution commit b96ea3a7925c18e8fb5f1da8fb3cf3a9d7c234ed to master and triggered hosted publish-images run #76.
- Hosted evidence so far: publish-catalog, validate-fast, validate-images, and all four publish-image matrix jobs passed; the run is currently blocked only in quay-security-gate step `Evaluate Quay security gate`.
- If context ends before completion, resume by polling run 25026661621 and then either fix the Quay/manifest stages if they fail or do final task bookkeeping and final push if the run passes.

- Hosted publish-images run #76 for commit b96ea3a7925c18e8fb5f1da8fb3cf3a9d7c234ed failed only in quay-security-gate after publish-catalog, validate-fast, validate-images, and all four publish-image jobs succeeded.
- Pulled the completed Quay gate logs: verify amd64 image quay.io/cockroach_migrate_tool/verify:b96ea3a7925c18e8fb5f1da8fb3cf3a9d7c234ed-amd64 hit policy_failure on advisory GHSA-j88v-2chj-qfwx with FixedBy 5.9.2; publish-manifest was skipped downstream.
- Created bug task .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-verify-image-amd64-has-quay-vulnerability-findings.md and should switch Ralph to the blocker task next, because story-29 task 04 is not complete while hosted verification still fails.
